mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easier discounting and lower equity hurdle rates, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because inflation surprise would offset the valuation channel.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month, or if the signal loses sign and Rank IC falls below 0.03 in easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which increases recession risk and pushes S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal should be visible after a discrete deterioration rather than a stable unemployment level.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if unemployment shocks are not followed by below-baseline returns within the next month.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in RSAFS alongside stronger HOUST reflects demand resilience and better revenue prospects for domestic firms, which supports S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if retail sales strength is not accompanied by housing improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST does not outperform either variable alone, or if average 20-day returns are not positive in the top joint-quartile of the two-series score.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates after a prior slowdown, it signals improving industrial output and stronger cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days. The effect is expected only after production has already been weak, because a rebound matters more than the absolute level.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after prior negative streak
failure_condition: Reject if positive reversals in INDPRO are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal is indistinguishable from noise in post-slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of CPIAUCSL re-acceleration and a FEDFUNDS rate that stays low creates an inflation surprise without offsetting policy restraint, which raises rate-volatility and compresses equity multiples over the next 5 trading days. The short-horizon effect is most likely around inflation print shocks.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by > 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint CPI-FEDFUNDS condition has no incremental predictive power over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS growth with a low UNRATE supports disposable income and earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is weaker when labor data are mixed, because the market then discounts the labor signal.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of payroll expansion and low unemployment do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal vanishes once unemployment rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in RSAFS relative to the prior month points to weakening consumption and near-term revenue pressure, which should drag S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when retail weakness appears after a prior rise, because reversals are more informative than persistent slow growth.
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign of the effect is positive in repeated triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and HOUST both improve together, the economy is moving out of a supply-side slowdown, supporting cyclical earnings and reducing recession odds, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint signal fails if only one series improves while the other remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST improvement does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day returns are not positive in the top joint-improvement bucket.
